Ntc And Motion For Order To Charge Scott Malek'S Interest In Solutions Wet Consulting, Llc
Off Calendar May-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Debra Reed's motion for an order to charge Scott Malek's interest in Solutions West Consulting LLC is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005, requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with two calendar days added if the motion was served by express mail or another method of delivery providing for overnight delivery. The motion was filed on April 16, 2008. Commissioner Bruce Chan. Not reported.

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-15-2007 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter came regularly on calendar this day, by assignment from the the Presiding Judge Dept. for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Commissioner Sue M. Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Bob Balian.
Michael T. Welch, Esq. for Plaintiff: Debra Reed, also present, was sworn and direct examined.
No appearance by the Defendant: Scott Malek.
After hearing the testimony and evidence presented, matter is submitted and taken under submission.
Counsel to prepare a Proposed Judgment.

Oct 15, 2007
Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant as follows:
Economic Damages $225,000.00
Emotional Distress Damages $500,000.00
Damages to Reputation $200,000.00
Punitive Damages Damages $250,000.00
Costs $5,458.00
Judgment signed by Comm. Kaplan.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Further Responses To, Or Compliance With, Discovery Requests
Off Calendar Jan-05-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael Welch for plaintiff and moving party: Debra Reed
Kristen Williams for Cross-defendant and opposing party: Wells Fargo Bank

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Debra Reed's motion to compel further responses to requests for admission, special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to request for admissions 12 and 13, request for production 5 and 8, and special interrogatory 5. The motion is granted as to the other requests. Defendant is ordered to produce documents and to identify with particularity any documents withheld based on a claim of privilege within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
